summaryText: Pastor Travis Hearn opened the service with prayer and addressed the topic of worry and anxiety, framing it as a common struggle that can strangle joy, peace, and faith. He explained that the English word 'worry' originates from an old word meaning 'to strangle or choke,' illustrating how chronic anxiety can control and damage our lives, health, and relationships. The core message was that God's Word provides practical solutions for our worries. Pastor Travis offered five key applications from Scripture: first, to remember that God deeply loves and cares for us, as illustrated in Matthew 6 where Jesus teaches us not to worry about daily needs because our Heavenly Father provides. Second, to remember that God is always with us, a truth highlighted in Psalm 23 and Deuteronomy 31:8, which promises God goes before us into our future. Third, to align our lives with God's Word, as walking in integrity and God's will minimizes self-inflicted anxiety. Fourth, instead of panicking, we are to pray, casting our cares on the Lord as instructed in Psalm 55:22 and Philippians 4:6-7. Fifth, we are to replace worrying with worship, refocusing our thoughts from our problems to God's power and presence. The sermon concluded with a call to surrender worries to God and an invitation to trust in Jesus.
mainPoints: Remember that God loves and cares for you, providing for your needs as a good Father.
Remember that God is always with you and has already gone before you into your future.
Instead of worrying, pray and worship, shifting your focus from your problems to God's power and presence.

versesMentioned.text: Anxiety weighs down the heart, but a kind word cheers it up.

Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes? Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they? Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life? And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labor or spin. Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these. If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith? So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’ For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them. But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well. Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
And my God will meet all your needs according to the riches of his glory in Christ Jesus.

The Lord is my shepherd, I lack nothing. He makes me lie down in green pastures, he leads me beside quiet waters, he refreshes my soul. He guides me along the right paths for his name’s sake. Even though I walk through the darkest valley, I will fear no evil, for you are with me; your rod and your staff, they comfort me.
The Lord himself goes before you and will be with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you. Do not be afraid; do not be discouraged.
Whoever walks in integrity walks securely, but whoever takes crooked paths will be found out.
Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him, and he will make your paths straight.
Adam and his wife were both naked, and they felt no shame.

When the woman saw that the fruit of the tree was good for food and pleasing to the eye, and also desirable for gaining wisdom, she took some and ate it. She also gave some to her husband, who was with her, and he ate it. Then the eyes of both of them were opened, and they realized they were naked; so they sewed fig leaves together and made coverings for themselves.
Cast your cares on the Lord and he will sustain you; he will never let the righteous be shaken.

Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God. And the peace of God, which transcends all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
Therefore, I urge you, brothers and sisters, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and pleasing to God—this is your true and proper worship.
When I am afraid, I put my trust in you. In God, whose word I praise—in God I trust and am not afraid. What can mere mortals do to me?

Monday.scripture: Matthew 6:25-26 (NIV) - 'Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life... Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?'
Monday.action: Identify one practical need you are worried about today. Write it down and consciously choose to trust God with it.
Monday.prayer: Father, thank you that you see my needs and value me. Help me to trust you as my provider today, especially with [mention the need].

Tuesday.scripture: Deuteronomy 31:8 (NIV) - 'The Lord himself goes before you and will be with you; he will never leave you nor forsake you.'
Tuesday.action: Before you start your day, take 2 minutes in silence to acknowledge God's presence with you. Remind yourself, 'God is with me.'
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, thank you for your promise to never leave me. Help me to walk in the confidence of your presence today, knowing you have already gone before me.

Wednesday.scripture: Proverbs 3:5-6 (NIV) - 'Trust in the Lord with all your heart and lean not on your own understanding; in all your ways submit to him...'
Wednesday.action: Evaluate one area of your life (e.g., a relationship, a habit, a decision) where you are leaning on your own understanding. Take one step to submit it to God's wisdom.
Wednesday.prayer: God, I submit my [mention the area] to you. Forgive me for leaning on my own understanding. Guide my steps and make my path straight as I trust in you.

Thursday.scripture: Philippians 4:6-7 (NIV) - 'Do not be anxious about anything, but in every situation, by prayer and petition, with thanksgiving, present your requests to God.'
Thursday.action: When you feel a wave of worry today, stop and immediately turn it into a short prayer of request and thanksgiving.
Thursday.prayer: Lord, instead of worrying about [mention the concern], I bring it to you right now. Thank you for hearing me. I receive your peace that guards my heart and mind.

Friday.scripture: Psalm 56:3-4 (NIV) - 'When I am afraid, I put my trust in you. In God, whose word I praise—in God I trust and am not afraid.'
Friday.action: Listen to a worship song on your way to work or during a break. Focus your thoughts on God's character, not your circumstances.
Friday.prayer: God, I choose to worship you. My fears are loud, but your faithfulness is greater. I praise you for your power, love, and sovereignty over my life.

Saturday.scripture: Psalm 55:22 (NIV) - 'Cast your cares on the Lord and he will sustain you; he will never let the righteous be shaken.'
Saturday.action: Write down the main burdens from your week on a piece of paper. In a moment of prayer, symbolically 'cast' them to God (e.g., tear it up, place it in a box).
Saturday.prayer: Heavenly Father, I am done carrying these burdens. I cast [name the burdens] onto you right now. I trust you to sustain me. Thank you for carrying my load.
